---
title: Gradio
lightbox: true
---

[Gradio](https://gradio.app/) makes it easy to build and share web apps, especially for ML demos. This guide shows you how to integrate querychat into your Gradio applications.

![](/images/gradio-app.png){fig-alt="Screenshot of querychat running in a Gradio app." class="lightbox shadow rounded mb-3"}

## Installation

Install querychat with Gradio support:
```bash
pip install "querychat[gradio]"
```

## Quick start

The simplest way to get started is with `.app()`, which creates a complete interface:

```{.python filename="gradio-app.py"}
from querychat.gradio import QueryChat
from querychat.data import titanic

qc = QueryChat(titanic(), "titanic")
app = qc.app()
app.launch()
```

Run with:
```bash
python gradio-app.py
```

## State management

Gradio uses a state-based architecture. The `.ui()` method returns a tuple of `(chat_column, state)`:

- **`chat_column`** - The chat UI component (a `gr.Column`)
- **`state`** - A `gr.State` containing the current query state as a dictionary

The state dictionary contains:

| Key | Type | Description |
|-----|------|-------------|
| `sql` | str \| None | Current SQL query |
| `title` | str \| None | Short description of current filter |
| `error` | str \| None | Error message if query failed |
| `chat_history` | list | Conversation history |

::: {.callout-tip}
Use the convenience methods `.df(state)`, `.sql(state)`, and `.title(state)` to access state values cleanly in your callbacks.
:::

## Custom layouts

Use `.ui()` with `gr.Blocks` for custom layouts:

```{.python filename="gradio-custom.py"}
import gradio as gr

from querychat.gradio import QueryChat
from querychat.data import titanic
from querychat.types import AppStateDict

qc = QueryChat(titanic(), "titanic")

with gr.Blocks() as app:
    gr.Markdown("# Titanic Explorer")

    with gr.Row():
        # Chat column (returned by .ui())
        chat_ui, state = qc.ui()

        # Custom right column
        with gr.Column():
            title_display = gr.Markdown("## All Data")
            data_table = gr.Dataframe(label="Data")
            sql_display = gr.Code(language="sql", label="SQL Query")

    # Update displays when state changes
    def update_display(state_dict: AppStateDict):
        df = qc.df(state_dict).to_pandas()
        sql = qc.sql(state_dict)
        title = qc.title(state_dict) or "All Data"

        return (
            f"## {title}",
            df,
            sql or f"SELECT * FROM {qc.data_source.table_name}"
        )

    state.change(
        fn=update_display,
        inputs=[state],
        outputs=[title_display, data_table, sql_display],
    )

qc.launch(app)
```

![](/images/gradio-custom-app.png){fig-alt="Screenshot of custom Gradio layout." class="lightbox shadow rounded mb-3"}

## Data views

Add visualizations that respond to query changes:

```{.python filename="gradio-viz.py"}
import gradio as gr
import plotly.express as px

from querychat.gradio import QueryChat
from querychat.data import titanic
from querychat.types import AppStateDict

qc = QueryChat(titanic(), "titanic")

with gr.Blocks() as app:
    with gr.Row():
        chat_ui, state = qc.ui()

        with gr.Column():
            data_table = gr.Dataframe(label="Data")
            plot = gr.Plot(label="Age Distribution")

    def update_views(state_dict: AppStateDict):
        df = qc.df(state_dict).to_pandas()

        fig = px.histogram(df, x="age", color="survived",
                          title="Age Distribution by Survival")
        return df, fig

    state.change(
        fn=update_views,
        inputs=[state],
        outputs=[data_table, plot],
    )

qc.launch(app)
```

![](/images/gradio-viz.png){fig-alt="Screenshot of Gradio data views example." class="lightbox shadow rounded mb-3"}

## Reset functionality

Add a reset button using Gradio's event system:

```{.python filename="gradio-reset.py"}
import gradio as gr

from querychat.gradio import QueryChat
from querychat.data import titanic
from querychat.types import AppStateDict

qc = QueryChat(titanic(), "titanic")

with gr.Blocks() as app:
    with gr.Row():
        chat_ui, state = qc.ui()

        with gr.Column():
            reset_btn = gr.Button("Reset Filters")
            data_table = gr.Dataframe(label="Data")

    def get_data(state_dict: AppStateDict):
        return qc.df(state_dict).to_pandas()

    def reset_data():
        return qc.data_source.get_data().to_pandas()

    state.change(fn=get_data, inputs=[state], outputs=[data_table])
    reset_btn.click(fn=reset_data, outputs=[data_table])

qc.launch(app)
```

![](/images/gradio-reset.png){fig-alt="Screenshot of Gradio reset functionality example." class="lightbox shadow rounded mb-3"}

## Complete example

Here's a full example with multiple views and controls:

```{.python filename="gradio-complete.py"}
import gradio as gr
import plotly.express as px

from querychat.gradio import QueryChat
from querychat.data import titanic
from querychat.types import AppStateDict

qc = QueryChat(titanic(), "titanic")

with gr.Blocks(title="Titanic Explorer") as app:
    gr.Markdown("# Titanic Dataset Explorer")

    with gr.Row():
        # Left: Chat interface
        chat_ui, state = qc.ui()

        # Right: Data views
        with gr.Column(scale=2):
            with gr.Row():
                title_display = gr.Markdown("## All Data")

            with gr.Row():
                passengers_box = gr.Textbox(label="Passengers", interactive=False)
                survivors_box = gr.Textbox(label="Survivors", interactive=False)
                rate_box = gr.Textbox(label="Survival Rate", interactive=False)

            with gr.Tabs():
                with gr.Tab("Data"):
                    data_table = gr.Dataframe()

                with gr.Tab("Visualization"):
                    plot = gr.Plot()

                with gr.Tab("SQL"):
                    sql_display = gr.Code(language="sql")

    def update_all(state_dict: AppStateDict):
        df = qc.df(state_dict).to_pandas()
        sql = qc.sql(state_dict)
        title = qc.title(state_dict) or "All Data"

        # Metrics
        n_passengers = str(len(df))
        n_survivors = str(int(df["survived"].sum()))
        survival_rate = f"{df['survived'].mean():.1%}"

        # Visualization
        fig = px.histogram(df, x="age", color="survived",
                          title="Age Distribution by Survival")

        # SQL
        sql_text = sql or f"SELECT * FROM {qc.data_source.table_name}"

        return (
            f"## {title}",
            n_passengers, n_survivors, survival_rate,
            df, fig, sql_text
        )

    state.change(
        fn=update_all,
        inputs=[state],
        outputs=[
            title_display,
            passengers_box, survivors_box, rate_box,
            data_table, plot, sql_display
        ],
    )

qc.launch(app)
```

![](/images/gradio-complete.png){fig-alt="Screenshot of complete Gradio app with querychat." class="lightbox shadow rounded mb-3"}

## Sharing on Hugging Face

Gradio apps can be easily shared on [Hugging Face Spaces](https://huggingface.co/spaces):

1. Create a new Space with Gradio SDK
2. Add your `app.py` and `requirements.txt`
3. Include your API key as a Space secret

```{.txt filename="requirements.txt"}
querychat[gradio]
```

## See also

- [Greet users](greet.qmd) - Create welcoming onboarding experiences
- [Provide context](context.qmd) - Help the LLM understand your data better
